dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: If it were any other way, I'd never forgive myself, nor you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Hah! Well then, may you recover as swiftly as ever, and I shall see you shortly on the other side. Farewell for now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Might I pay you a visit tonight during your return? If you'd welcome that...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...Perhaps. You know where to find me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: I'd like to see you again this evening, once you're finished with your work of course.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...I think that can be arranged.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: I mustn't interfere too much with your journey tonight... but, might I see you after?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...Yes, you certainly might.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Should your path lead to an untimely fall tonight, perhaps I can be there to catch you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...You're welcome to try.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Do you think you might be free this evening once your work is over with?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...Oh I think so, yes.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: When you next return to shadow, I'd like to join you again, if you'll have me.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...I'd like that, too.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: ...You summoned me?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Good evening, Lord Moros. Glad you could make it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: ...As much as I long for you to succeed each night, I couldn't wait to see you again.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I felt much the same. See you in the Crossroads.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Pardon the interruption.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: There you are, Moros. It's good to see you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I'm so accustomed to feeling alone while here... but to be together with you...?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's nice. Though we had best get back.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Please don't return just yet.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You got here just in time, Lord Moros. Good.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: ...It's comfortable here, isn't it? Peaceful and quiet. And with you, so much the better.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's comfortable indeed. We could stay longer I suppose?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: ...All these shadows.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Hi, Moros. I suppose we're all alone again.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I know it's not your preference to end up here in this place, but we can make it ours.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'd say we already have.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Moonlight even here.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You made it, Lord Moros. And always so punctual.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: ...If you're not in a hurry to return, would you mind remaining here with me?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It would be my pleasure.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Pardon the intrusion.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Hello, Moros. I've thought fondly of when last we were here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I used to feel nothing while here. But now, with you... there's so much more.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: And I look forward to meeting my Doom on nights like this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: I thank the Fates you're safe with us, Princess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: You'll be pleased to know I've no additional bad news to share, for now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: May the Fates be favorable on your next attempt.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm beginning to acclimate to these Crossroads, I think!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm managing, Princess, though this is very strange for me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Who can say what the Fates have in store for any of us now...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: The Fates entrusted you with so much, Princess...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: I'll do my best to make myself useful around here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: It's a pleasure to see you again, as always, Princess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: I regret the setbacks you've encountered, though at least you're here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Please don't hesitate to let me know if I can be of service in some way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: May you be well-prepared for whatever is to come.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Remember that the future is yet to be woven, Princess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: I've so much left to learn about this way of life...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Do let me know if there's something I could do to ease your burden even for a bit.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: I trust your task is growing ever closer to completion, Princess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Some of the Shades here still don't seem particularly fond of me, I'm afraid...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: What would the Fates have me do next, I wonder...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Thank you for making me feel welcome here, Princess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: If the Fates have taught me anything, it's that our lives can take the strangest turns...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Be well, Princess Melinoë, and may my sisters always favor you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Always a pleasure, Princess. Though, please... don't let me keep you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Take heart knowing the Fates themselves believe that you'll succeed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Go bring those foes of ours some more bad news for me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Princess Melinoë. May doom befall your foes.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: I seldom get to say we meet again, yet here we are, Princess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: To think that we were fated to meet under such circumstances...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm certain you've more urgent matters to attend to than keeping company with me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Forgive me, Princess. I... I need some time to think.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: I've no additional bad news for now, I'm pleased to say, Princess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: May your next battle beyond space and time go as smoothly as the last.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: You need a few extra moments out there, remember you have the Engraved Pin.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: I shall be keeping an eye on the Fated List, in case of any sudden change.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: If you're headed toward the surface, perhaps I can delay Eris a bit...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: I know you've done this once or twice, though please be careful nonetheless.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Each time I see you, it reminds me of the first, Melinoë.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps I ought to take up weaving for myself with all of this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm beginning to fear your companion Dora is set to sneak up on me...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Come what may, I'm pleased that I could be here in these Crossroads with you all.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Princess, we've not met formally. I'm called Moros, Doom, or the official Bearer of Bad News. The Witch of the Crossroads apprised me of the situation here, and was gracious to invite me to stay for now.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You're welcome here and safe, Lord Moros. So then, you know of my task.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: To vanquish the usurper of the House of Hades seems more than a task. I regret this burden is yours to bear, and much sooner than expected.

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: It's all right. I was born for this.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Say, Lord Moros, would you accompany me to the riverside? I've a feeling your mere presence may help with the evening's catch.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: I have a way with mortal creatures of all sorts, so I'll accompany you, yes.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Forgive me, though I never have quite understood the compulsion to extract water-creatures from their natural environs. I know for mortals it is sometimes necessary sustenance, though others do it for the sake of sport. Why?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: For mortals, it seems to be an opportunity to assert themselves over the natural world. Though I would take no pride in outsmarting such creatures if I were them. In our case... we safeguard habitats for all the species that we find.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: That seems like a burdensome responsibility for creatures that ought to take better care of themselves.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well, there's more to it, I suppose. For one thing it sets a contemplative mood, right? Means we can talk a little about matters unrelated to our own burdensome responsibilities.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: I can certainly appreciate that part. And now we wait...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps it's good to let the mind wander a bit to such pursuits, every once in a while.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I don't suppose you'd join me for a little while at the fishing pier, Lord Moros? Thought we could use these Twin Lures.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah! I fear the river denizens will not approach this evening if I'm there, for they can sense me every now and then. Apologies.

options.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
options.text: Certainly, as long as you don't mind if I scare off everything in the river.

options.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
options.text: Fair warning, you'll get no unfair advantages from me luring those unsuspecting fish.

options.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
options.text: Apparently the time has come for a river denizen or two.

options.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
options.text: We may endure some ridicule from Eris, though why not.

options.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
options.text: ...I don't know what it is about me that fish find so imposing...

options.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
options.text: ...I quite enjoy it over by the riverside. Thank you for the outing.

options.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
options.text: ...A most successful outing, and the night is just getting started!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Lord Moros, as our honored guest, erm, we wanted you to have this. To make your stay a bit more comfortable.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: The Nectar of the gods! Thank you, Princess. I've... not received an offering before. Though I do like to come prepared. So here! From me to you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Huh! I must admit I'm unaccustomed to displays of flattery. Forgive me, I don't quite know what to say, except... thank you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You're very welcome! Though, I'd not intended it as flattery, it's just.... Don't even mention it, all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: You're very kind to me, Princess. I arrived at these Crossroads in a desperate state. I didn't expect to be made welcome here.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: And I didn't expect to meet Doom face-to-face, much less for him to be rather agreeable. Yet here we are!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Why, thank you for this, truly, though you needn't go out of your way for me. You've been so generous. Forgive my asking, but... this isn't due to some sort of prophecy in the Fated List, is it?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: No! No, of course not, Lord Moros! Although, even if it was, I would be duty-bound not to inform you, wouldn't I?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm honored to accept this, Princess Melinoë. When the Fates first indicated that we'd meet, I asked them what I should expect; what you were like. Clotho smiled and simply said, you'll see. I felt certain they were playing a prank!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You make me glad I don't have any sisters, Lord Moros. So what would you have said in Clotho's place?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh! Perhaps I would have said... she's a great witch and enchantress. The Princess of the Underworld, daughter to the goddess of spring and the lord of the dead. Though whatever you imagine her to be... she's more.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: This Nectar of the gods, it's... such a luxury. With my sisters, I led a relatively simple life. Now to find myself in such a place, much less with you... no prophecy could have prepared me.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You seem to truly like it here. I think we have our needs fulfilled, though I've not often heard anything in the Crossroads described as luxurious. Are things so different where you're from?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, it was an excellent place to be a weaver. But there was not a lot of other recreational activity, or other company. I fear I may become quite self-indulgent with all this...

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: I don't think you're at any risk of that just yet.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I wanted you to have this, Lord Moros, since you seemed to enjoy it last time. Ambrosia may flow freely on Olympus, but we chthonic gods should get our share as well, wouldn't you say?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh! I think that's a better question for Nemesis. It's not for me to say whether we deserve such things. And the Fates did not advise me as to whether I should accept such gifts. Nor did I ever think to ask...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I can't believe I'm saying this, though there's no need to overthink it so much. Since the Fates aren't here to tell you, you could always listen to your heart in such a situation...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: My heart... it isn't saying anything, Princess. But it is... beating faster than I'm accustomed, even though I'm at rest, and comfortable, mostly. Very comfortable! Ah, I don't wish to be so rude, so thank you very much.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: If I've learned anything it's that with practice comes perfection, or as close as we can ever get. So, what do you say we practice listening to our hearts a little more? Here, this is for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm... I'm attempting to listen to my heart right now, Princess. But if truly it can speak, I'm afraid it's speaking a bit rapidly, such that I can't entirely understand or know what to do... as though I'm in one of your binding spells.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: How about I go first, and next I offer you Ambrosia, you can be prepared. For now, my heart says... I feel comfortable around you. Even though you make me feel like royalty, which come to think of it, I do not mind at all.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: I... thank you very much for that, Princess. I'm pleased that you approve of me this way. And, next time, as you say, I'll... be better prepared to express my sentiment, in turn.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well, Lord Moros, ready to let your heart do the talking this time? Because here's something that I wanted you to have. Your calm and considerate presence in these Crossroads has meant a lot to me, so thank you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: I should be thanking you instead, Princess. Not merely for this gift, which is beautiful... but also because you've given me something else to strive for, besides the Fate-appointed calling that I've known.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Something to strive for? You mean the Nectar and Ambrosia? Good, isn't it? I should be able to get more!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: I'd welcome that, although it isn't what I meant, which is... knowing you could spend your time with anyone, I'll strive to make the time you spend with me worthwhile. As fulfilling for you as it is for me.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: But I already find the time we share fulfilling. I wouldn't want you thinking otherwise, or changing how you are with me. I like the way I feel when I'm with you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: And you don't know how glad I am to hear it. Well, I've relayed what I believe my heart has said, after a closer listening. Perhaps I ought have kept its message to myself...

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: No, of course not. I'm glad.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Something unusual occurred, Princess. The Fated List scrolled to a point that caught my eye. What it said made little sense to me though I'm implored to tell you: I see your spirits glimmer as the metals underneath the Earth.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I see your spirits glimmer as the metals underneath the Earth. That sounds much like a waking-phrase! Headmistress told me of them when she lent me the Nocturnal Arms. When you think on it, what do you see...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: A daemon of some sort. A mask-like face, in shadow, some nocturnal realm, not necessarily our own. Who is this?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: We'll soon find out I think. Likely a bearer of the Arms of Night, but whether past or future, I can't say.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: I may have just fulfilled part of a prophecy in relaying those words. I trust they'll be of benefit, not harm?

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: I think so. I see your spirits glimmer as the metals underneath the Earth...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: I have something for you, Princess. Where I reside, such Darkness was quite commonplace, but perhaps not anymore. Here, in case you need it.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh, thank you, Lord Moros! Here in the Crossroads we get just enough moonlight that Darkness such as this is almost never seen. Wait... did you fulfill a prophecy just now?

endLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
endLines.text: I would have given it to you even if not.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Unnamed_01
dialogueLines.text: Princess of the Underworld: I regret having to contact you at such a time. It's not my custom to intervene during moments of Doom, only to observe.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Moments of Doom.... You're Lord Moros, emissary of the Three Fates! So your sisters heard me after all!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: They expected me to find you, for there's something they intend for you to have. Wherever you're going, look for it when you arrive. I'm uncertain if we'll meet again; farewell.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: ...You traveled to the surface. Knowing full well the Fates decided long ago that those of your line could not survive there, and would always be forced back to the Underworld.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Their curse was... palpable. I never intended to stay for long. Just long enough to lend my relatives support. But it seems your sisters insist that I do no such thing.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Well... I don't see them around right now, do you? Incidentally, there was once an incantation that I happened upon, which was not intended for my eyes. But I now choose to think that it was intended for yours.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: My sisters... they're all right! I was about to say thank the Fates, but in this case, Princess? I can only say thank you. While you'd best not tell me how you did it, please do tell how it was to meet them finally...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It was an interesting conversation! And they seem to be doing well, that much I can say. And you're probably aware of the new major prophecy that they foretold.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: The advent of another age! They notified me, yes. Perhaps it's their way of preventing any further attempts to circumvent their will. Assuming this age does arrive.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What does this mean for you, though, Moros? I got the sense your sisters have no immediate plans to resume their customary weavings.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: I think it means that mortals shall be having a reprieve from me, potentially for quite some time. And that I'll have to find something to do...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You could remain here with us. Help tend the Crossroads. Learn our craft.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: This advent of another age your sisters have described... do you think it's possible Prometheus anticipated it? When I think back on things he said...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: You're asking if the Titan of Foresight knew the Fates would eventually make this choice, before even they knew it. Hm. Perhaps the lot of them conspired to achieve a shared goal...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Lord Moros! I'm surprised you would even suggest such things! To have conspired against the gods, and Chronos for that matter!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: I merely seek alternatives to the suggestion that my sisters may not be the first to know. So then Prometheus prevailed on behalf of mortals after all. Or shall, when comes this age.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Sometimes I don't know what to do with myself anymore, Princess. I thought if you succeeded in your task that I'd return to my responsibilities. But now it seems I won't.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm familiar with that feeling. I've been so fixated on my goal that I hadn't really thought about what's next... but here we are still, right? The task wasn't the end.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: So what are we supposed to do? I'm Doom Incarnate; I'll never be known for my fishing, for instance. I fear... perhaps my best work is behind me.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I fear that too, though it's nonsense. We have the rest of our existence ahead of us. Mortals have but the blink of an eye.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
endLines.text: We have it easier than mortals, certainly.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: You were wielding what must have been the Flames of Ygnium but in a form I didn't know. I felt them... call to me. So I am here.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Lord Moros, you sensed the Aspect of the Arms of Night bearing your name. The Flames already recognize you, but... I think I mustn't give them to you yet.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh I don't want them... I've never had to fight in such a way. Seldom even used my pinpoint blade, thank the Fates. Though perhaps when all this is over, you can teach me how to use them. See you at the Crossroads, Princess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: I stalled your doom as long as I was able to, Princess. But it was not sufficient. Forgive me... the Engraved Pin I gave you has its limitations, I'm afraid.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I got farther than I would have without it, Lord Moros. So I'm grateful to you and to this power you've bestowed. I know I could have put it to better use.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: I know the same. Perhaps the reason I am here is so that you might reach your destination with this Pin. Or discover that you don't require it. Now let us both return...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: The Engraved Pin helped you withstand your foes for longer even than I thought it could. Your bond with it is strong... meaning, our bond. But it was not enough.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I think I'm supposed to be the one feeling frustrated right now, Lord Moros. This certainly isn't the Pin's fault.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, nevertheless. May I escort you back to the Crossroads at least? I know returning can be quite abrupt, but I can mitigate a bit of that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Your task is finally complete...! Perhaps the Fates knew you'd succeed, but I couldn't always be certain. Although I held to what could be considered hope.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: And your support made it possible, Lord Moros. For one thing, if you hadn't intervened when I was trying to reach the surface, Typhon might have reduced Olympus to ruin.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Doom averted...! We children of Nyx have already heard that Mother is no longer bound in Tartarus; you have our deepest gratitude, certainly mine. Well then... what happens now?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...I suppose I ought to go make certain everything's in order. My father started renovations to the Underworld, and I've volunteered to help clean up. The surface needs it too.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
endLines.text: Then it sounds like I may see you again soon?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: How fares your effort to cleanse all of the disorder Chronos wrought? I trust this new task doesn't fall solely to you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh, everybody's pitching in, even Chronos... my grandfather. You heard what happened to him, right? His new self seems more tolerable than before.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Death to Chronos... to his old self. I don't know how you managed it, Princess. Perhaps not even my sisters could know such a thing, wherever they may be.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: We ought to find where they've been exiled, and I don't think Chronos can simply tell us. What about you, perhaps enough has changed that you can locate them somehow?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: I often search the void where you and I first met, since the Fates would sometimes go there, too. But it's a vast place...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Again our journeys intertwine. Why did you call upon me, Princess? Did you not receive the artifact I brought you from the Fates?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Lord Moros...! I did receive the Fated List, and summoned you once more as it ordained. All children of Nyx are welcome where I reside. Might we speak further there...? I've many questions.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: I don't know whether I can answer them, as my own search for truth has not been going well of late. But I'll not decline your cordial invitation. Please lead the way.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Lord Moros? I wanted you to have this either way, though if you're not too busy at the moment, would you care to share it with me at the taverna? I know this is sudden, it's just... I'm past due for a little break...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Princess Melinoë, it would be my pleasure. Would you please lead the way?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...So, it can be a lot, of course. I don't know what's the better source of motivation, those completely certain I'll succeed in everything, or those completely certain that I'll fail.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Hah! I have the same thought pattern frequently. The Fates, they could anticipate the outcomes of my efforts and would chastise me sometimes for small mistakes I'd yet to make. It wasn't always as encouraging as intended.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Then, where did you find the strength? Your responsibilities sound like they must have been a lot.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: It all simply became a force of habit at some point. You practice a skill to a level of proficiency, then fear letting it go... fear you'll never be as capable at other things...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I wouldn't have expected Doom himself to harbor such misgivings.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Well! Perhaps I should have been born as Fear! So then where do you think it comes from? Your source of motivation for your task.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I've decided it comes from the thought of finding more of this Ambrosia out there; this is good!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: I very much enjoyed your company, Princess, and thank you for the drink. I trust we'll have more opportunities like this?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Forgive me for presuming, though you seem a little tense of late, Lord Moros. What would you say if we shared one of these bottles together at the taverna? It's been a while.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Nothing would please me more, Princess... except, it's not my wish to burden you, and I regret that I've permitted my concerns, which are slight, to become apparent. So please, don't go out of your way on my account.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm not going out of my way. And if it's truly your wish for the time we share to be fulfilling, then you can start by taking the opportunities that present themselves.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: You make a strong case. It would be my honor to accompany you, then. Shall we head down?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I have been drawn to others on occasion, yes. But, I knew better than to let myself become attached. If my heart started to speak to me, I always could make it quiet down.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: In that, at least, we're quite alike... skilled at quieting our hearts. Though if the Lady Aphrodite's taught me anything, it's that our passions needn't always be suppressed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: What do you mean, Princess? What passions could you have possibly suppressed, given all that you've achieved?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well, for one thing, even being here with you... the past me never would have had the clarity of mind to ask, I think. My mind would have talked my heart out of it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Then I'm very grateful that your heart is beginning to prevail in more of these debates. I think it only makes you stronger, and... perhaps it's the Ambrosia, but... I'm very fond of that. Of you in your entirety, truly.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh...! While we're being candid, then... I've become rather fond of you as well... but, if you're promised to another, I don't wish to interfere.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: I don't know this other goddess to whom I've been promised, nor do I want to. And I've violated the will of the Fates already; we both have. Wherever all this leads, I would have you by my side.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: I very much enjoyed our time, Princess, and will anticipate when we can be together next. Soon, I trust.

options.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
options.text: Princess, it would be my pleasure to share some Ambrosia with you.

options.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
options.text: As long as you don't think I'd be unwelcome over there, especially with you.

options.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
options.text: I could do with some time away from the Fated List, now that you mention it.

options.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
options.text: ...The other Shades still seem a bit on edge with me around...

options.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
options.text: ...This truly is one of my favorite customs here. To your continued victories!

options.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
options.text: ...At times like this, I think about my sisters, without whom we never would have met.

options.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
options.text: Thank you for the company. I trust you enjoyed that as much as I did.

options.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
options.text: How is it that Ambrosia can taste different and amazing every time?

options.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
options.text: I wonder if the Fates themselves knew we would have times such as these.

options.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
options.text: Thank you for that! I had a pleasant time with you back there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Nevertheless, I feel as though I ought have been there, Brother. Perhaps I could have protected them as was my charge. To think the Fates themselves may have denied me the attempt. It's discomforting that I may never know.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Keeehhhh, hraaauuggghhh...!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: You have every reason to be angry. I'm angry, too. Nonetheless, I'm grateful that our paths have intertwined again. Be safe, Charon.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Charon... you, Nemesis, even Hypnos. Who else, Eris? It seems I was one of the last to know of these Crossroads among the children of Nyx. Perhaps I ought have stayed in better contact.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Mrrnngggghhh, hnnnn...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Come, Lord Charon, your brother is our guest. And even if you did all drift apart, you're here together now. Moving forward.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
endLines.text: Wait, how did you...? No matter.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Come to your senses, Eris. You're not doing yourself any favors by impeding the will of the Fates. Have you heard of even a single incident where that turned out for the best?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: No! But there's a first time for everything, and I'm not interested in hearing veiled threats from them through you. If they care so much about what I'm up to, why don't they contact me themselves?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: You know that isn't how it works. Have you truly made up your mind, to side against... well, everyone? Eugh, even I'm not supposed to be speaking to you...

endLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
endLines.text: I do what I want, Moros.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Come on, man! Gotta tell me why you're really here! The Three don't just send you out on long assignments like this! Wait... is it because of her?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Please, Eris. The Princess ought to be addressed as such. And lest you forget, the details of my arrival are not for me to divulge. There must be something else we can discuss.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I can think of lots of things. I want to know what's going on with you two. I see the way you look up at her as she trots by. Maybe the Three sent you here to set the two of you up...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: You're trying to embarrass me... it isn't going to work. And besides, we both know the Fates have better uses of their weaving-time than things like that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Do they really? Thought they were all about meddling with the smallest details of everybody's lives! But, you'd know better than me.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
endLines.text: I know better than to be provoked like this...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: ...As long as it is not an imposition, Lady Hecate. I know you have far greater responsibilities than looking after yet another child of Nyx. Especially when Nyx herself is back.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: When Melinoë first summoned you I said that you could stay for now, and it continues to be now, does it not? You seem the sort to make yourself useful without getting underfoot. 'Tis all that I require.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Then I shall do so to the best of my ability. Please notify me if you deem my performance unsatisfactory in any way.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
endLines.text: Oh gods, you're just like her.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Zzzzz, zzzz...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: How strange to be encountering this brother of mine here. So then he truly hasn't stirred at all?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Not once. Headmistress Hecate says Hypnos was already fast asleep when she recovered him from my father's House. You never visited him there before?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: It wasn't my place. We each come from big families, don't we? Odd families. You know how it is.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Oh yes, I certainly remember you. Would it be any consolation knowing that the Fates had already decided that your wings would fail you, and you would fall?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Erm, not exactly, no. For one thing, that would make the Three Fates seem a little, well, you know... mean. I was just some kid, and an imprisoned kid! Why pick on me? And for another, are you saying that I had no real choice?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Well first, my sisters have to be impartial, to keep a healthy distance from their work. Rarely is it ever personal. As for the choice you made... it was still yours, except... the outcome would have likely happened either way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: You're saying if I didn't fly up like a dolt and melt my wings, they would have simply broken or something later on, and I'd have plunged into the water anyway...?

endLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
endLines.text: Not necessarily exactly that... but yes.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: To be direct with you, Master Odysseus, I am what you might call an admirer. At first, I found you extraordinarily frustrating, so adept were you at avoiding certain Doom. But gradually, perhaps grudgingly, I grew very impressed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, hah, I'm... flattered and relieved to hear it, very kind of you to let me know, particularly after... all this time...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: It's quite humorous, in fact. While you drew breath, I never once had opportunity to tell you!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Erm, quite humorous indeed, milord! I intended no offense by avoiding you, you know that, of course? A mortal impulse, whilst in mortal peril.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, you were a wonder to behold! There were the Laestrygonian Giants, Charybdis, and, lest we forget... the war of Ilium...!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: ...So then... were you ever certain you had finally met your Doom? Or did you truly think there was always a plan of escape to be discovered, no matter how dire the circumstance?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, to be quite frank with you, Moros — may we be informal thus? I had the hardest time untangling myself not from near-fatal situations, but the arms of certain goddesses... my greatest weakness, looking back...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, indeed... but how was this a weakness? As I understand, your wife Penelope stood by your side... I trust I do not overstep; I know this can be a sensitive topic with those from the surface.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Aye, Penelope stood by my side, having spent her best years awaiting a man who was not always in a hurry to return. After we each lived out our days, we reunited in the Underworld, but... we needed a break.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: I see. After everything you'd been through, did your relationship end in an amicable way, at least?

endLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
endLines.text: It didn't end so much as changed, but... aye.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: ...The wooden horse was an exquisite trick. Your own inevitable Doom, misdirected toward your unsuspecting foes! A masterstroke. But my personal favorite had to be the Polyphemus incident...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: You speak of it like an old action yarn. Big bastard massacred my crew. My heart threatened to burst from my chest getting past him, and now he's up to his old antics with the Goddess there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, but I have no doubt that with your guidance, the Princess shall outsmart the Cyclops again and again. As recompense for the trouble that he caused you!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Oh we were very busy during that time, as I know you were, Master Odysseus. Marshaling armies, outsmarting enemy commanders... you fought so fiercely then!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Yes, well... I'm pleased to now be plying my trade from behind a desk. It's difficult to lead a life of bloodshed for too long. Especially for mortals since they die.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Well I salute all your accomplishments and daring escapes! Lady Hecate could not have chosen a better Shade.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
endLines.text: Hah, surely not if you yourself say so!
